title: Four Against Darkness - Deep Dive Part 5 - The Lantern Zine Bundle
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2lZK2lUb6ew

Daniel (@dungeondive) waxes genuinely nostalgic about The Lantern, a community-run zine that encapsulates everything charming about the DIY ethos surrounding Four Against Darkness. Available directly through Andrea (the game's creator) for $20-30, The Lantern Issues 1 and 2 represent a labor of love: handwritten notes, exclusive rule additions unique to each copy, beautiful pen-and-ink illustrations, and enough gameplay content to fuel weeks of adventuring. This is the kind of passion project Daniel remembers from his youth—something to treasure and carry everywhere.

Issue 1 includes an introductory adventure to capture gnome treasure, an adventure originally printed on a T-shirt, the wonderfully-chaotic "Blimmies" minion variant, and pre-set thematic adventures like "Four Against the Goblin King" that demonstrate house-ruling mechanics. Issue 2 escalates with "The Mists of Warunor," a genuine campaign featuring the town of Warner, NPCs with personalities, a bulletin board for side quests, and a meta-narrative where players must gather clues to understand what's threatening the region. It's complimented by the "Mushroom Menace" (evoking Mertwwig's Maze with its branching-tree dungeon layout), settlement raid mechanics, and gorgeous card decks of unique bosses, minions, vermin, and weird monsters.

Beyond the physical and mechanical offerings, The Lantern represents something precious in modern tabletop gaming: a tangible connection to creators and community that mass-produced supplements can't replicate.

----

Have you discovered any beloved zines or independent supplements that captured your imagination in similar ways?
